title: astrantia major subsp. involucrata
intro: Astrantia major subsp. involucrata, also known as Masterwort, is a perennial plant in the Apiaceae family that is native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems. The medicinal properties of Masterwort are attributed to its rich composition of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been shown to have antioxidant, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. Herbal preparations of Masterwort can be consumed as teas, tinctures, or infusions, and have been used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support overall health and well-being.
